root_cause: OCS had certified per-stage replay reconstruction but lacked a single read-only inspection runtime that reconstructed and summarized the complete cognition chain for operators.
runtime_model: OCS chain inspection consumes explicit OCS context, cognition, replay-derived intent, memory, continuity, semantic resolution, and OCS-to-PPP handoff artifacts.
The runtime validates replay visibility, artifact hashes, known OCS artifact types, known OCS statuses, and full source lineage hash continuity.
The runtime rejects any source carrying authority-bearing flags.
The runtime reconstructs ordered OCS chain stages.
The runtime displays continuity links, semantic resolution results, replay-derived intent candidates, and PPP handoff candidates.
The runtime creates a deterministic operator summary.
The runtime marks all outputs as read-only, proposal-only, and non-authoritative.
The inspection hash is derived from source hashes, chain stages, continuity links, semantic results, candidates, operator summary, authority flags, status, and failure state.
Replay reconstruction validates ordering, wrapper hashes, artifact hashes, returned continuity, and inspection hash continuity.
replay_impact: OCS now has append-only replay-visible chain inspection evidence.
Replay reconstruction can prove which OCS artifacts shaped the operator inspection summary.
Identical OCS chains produce the same inspection hash.
The runtime does not mutate source replay.
The runtime does not invoke PPP, providers, workers, approval, governance mutation, implementation, or execution.
